## Part 4
- West region contributes the  most revenue
- Electronics category performs best.
- The sales consistently declined across the dates.
- When sales decline, quickly identify where the drop is coming from and which metric changed most—traffic, conversion rate, average order value, or repeat purchases—then fix the biggest “leak” first. Reconnect with recent and lost customers to confirm the real reasons, monitor competitor pricing and promotions, and run short, targeted actions like win-back campaigns, bundles/upsells, improved follow-up, and limited-time value offers rather than permanent discounts. At the same time, focus spending on the highest-ROI channels, tighten costs that don’t drive revenue, and execute a simple 30–60–90 day plan with clear owners and KPIs to stabilize, optimize, and then grow.



Lab Questions
Part A
1) Date, Product, Category, Sales, Region
2) Whole number was assigned to the Sales on the raw date and the instruction told us to alter it to Decimal.
3) Data view
4) Its the line chart you can easily adentify the trends.
5) Sum, it adds up all Sales values for whatever context the visual has.

Part B
6) West region contributes the  most revenue
7) Office supplies
8) decreasing across the years
9) Prioritise North cause the sales are low
10) North is earning the least money, and Office Supplies is not selling well in all areas. Fix Office Supplies for everyone by making sure the best items are always available, removing items that don’t sell, and encouraging bigger purchases using bundles (group items together) and “buy more, save more” deals. At the same time, increase sales in North by checking what’s causing the drop (not enough customers, customers not buying, or orders too small) and then doing North-only actions like contacting past customers, running ads only in North, and offering simple deals like free shipping if they spend a certain amount. Track weekly if sales go up and keep doing what works best.
